Turn 75 ‚ÄĒ notation firewall and stack-rank normalization before final appendix freeze I used only the three files attached to this turn. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients defined in the problem statement, namely whether there is a continuous isomorphism őďS‚ÜíőďH commuting with the canonical maps to G.The attached history has already frozen the structural proof: residual induction, coefficient Koszul‚ÄďWitt induction, nonlinear Certificates D.1‚ÄďD.4, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness give an over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF with ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH, hence őďS‚ČÖGőďH.The useful Turn 75 move is not another obstruction computation. It is to remove the last presentation hazard in the appendix: the same 2√ó2 maps have been described in both ‚Äúbasis-action‚ÄĚ and ‚Äúordinary right-row matrix‚ÄĚ notation across the final audit turns. That can silently transpose U0 and N. The proof is not affected, but the manuscript appendix should forbid the ambiguity.

1. Use row operators as the primary notation

Since relation rows are right őõ=Z2 [[G]]-rows, define the dangerous Ef-piece H1 -move as the row operatorU0 (a,b)=(a(s‚ąí1)+b(s‚ąí2),¬†a+2b).ThenU0 (s+3,‚ąí2)=((s+3)(s‚ąí1)‚ąí2(s‚ąí2),¬†(s+3)‚ąí4)=(s2+1,¬†s‚ąí1),and the determinant is 2(s‚ąí1)‚ąí(s‚ąí2)=s,a unit. This is exactly the Ef-piece H1 -Nielsen move used to glue the global U‚ąąGL2 (őõ) carrying wS=(ŌÉ+3E,‚ąí2) to wH=(Eg0+3E‚ąí2,ŌÉ‚ąíE).If an ordinary row-multiplication matrix is printed, it must beU0row=( s‚ąí1s‚ąí212),(a,b)U0row=U0 (a,b).If the appendix instead prints a basis-action display, it must explicitly say so. The safe rule is: never print U0 as a naked array without saying whether it is a row matrix or a basis-action matrix.

2. Do the same for the coefficient normalization

The local Ef coefficient row is(s2+1,¬†s‚ąí1),t=s‚ąí1,and the exact normalization is(s2+1)‚ąí(s+1)(s‚ąí1)=2.

So define the coefficient shear as the row operator N(a,b)=(a‚ąí(s+1)b,¬†b).ThenN(s2+1,¬†s‚ąí1)=(2,t).

If an ordinary right-row matrix is printed, it must beNrow=(1‚ąí(s+1)01).This exact normalization is the entry point for the coefficient proof: after it, the coefficient row is the regular Koszul row (2,t), and the normalized Koszul‚ÄďWitt induction closes the full Ef-local coefficient tower.

3. Normalize the stack-rank notation The Certificate D rank statements should also be written as maps to direct sums, not as ambiguous vertical arrays.For D.1, defineS10:L8‚äēL8‚ü∂L9‚äēL5[2],S10 (u,v)=(‚ąā8 (u,v),¬†B10 (u,v)).

Then the certificate is rank(‚ąā8)=56,rank(S10)=62.

Since dimL5[2]=6, this provesB10 (ker‚ąā8)=L5[2].That is the invariant version of the degree-10 Bockstein witness package.For D.3, define

V:=3L5‚äē3L6‚äē3L7andS4:V‚ü∂T6‚äēT7‚äēT8‚äēF2,S4 (z)=(D5,6,7 z,¬†őľ4B4 (z)).

Then the certificate is rank(D5,6,7)=60,rank(S4)=61.

Since H4(2)is one-dimensional, this is precisely the statement that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed őľ4=1 scalar to 0. The attached history records the executable D.3 output verifying the rank jump 60‚Üí61.

4. D.2 and D.4 remain scalar/witness certificates D.2 should remain a scalar transgression certificate, not a giant coordinate table. The lower-base word iső∑4=Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4))).

The verifier contract is:S(Y0(8)ő∑4,Y1(8),q(8))‚Č°X2 (modI9),the fixed degree-16 class has őĽ=1, the lower-deformed class has őĽ=0, and the high torsor image is kerőĽ. This certifies that the degree-16 high-torsor codimension-one miss is not invariant under lower-base freedom. The history repeatedly identifies this ‚Äúfixed gauge gives a false obstruction, full torsor kills it‚ÄĚ pattern as structural rather than case-specific.D.4 remains the short quotient witness:[B,B8]=0,őł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2],őĽ((B8)[2])=1.

Hence kerőĽ+imőł80=L8[2],so the residual d=8 codimension-one miss disappears inH8(2)=L8[2]/imőł80.This is already the minimal certificate; expanding it further would obscure the structural reason it works.

5. Final appendix wording

The final appendix should open with:All relation rows are right őõ-rows. All Nielsen moves used in row computations are first defined as row operators. Ordinary matrix arrays are printed only after specifying this convention.

Then use the following normalized objects:U0 (a,b)=(a(s‚ąí1)+b(s‚ąí2),a+2b),N(a,b)=(a‚ąí(s+1)b,b),S10 (u,v)=(‚ąā8 (u,v),B10 (u,v)),S4 (z)=(D5,6,7z,őľ4B4 (z)).With that notation, Certificates 0‚ÄďF are presentation-safe. The compactness argument then uses finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle solution sets SN,J, not arbitrary finite quotient automorphisms; each finite datum has invertible first layer UmodJ, hence gives a finite over-G automorphism, and compactness gives the desired inverse-limit automorphism of F.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 75 removes the last notation hazard. The final appendix should define U0, N, D.1, and D.3 as row operators or direct-sum stack maps, not as ambiguous naked matrices. With these conventions, the certificate proof remains unchanged and yields a continuous over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF with ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH, hence őďS‚ČÖGőďH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The remaining work is external reproducibility/presentation: attach the actual D.1 and D.2 pivot tables or verifier scripts, while using the row-operator and stack-rank conventions above to avoid transpose ambiguity.

NEXT: Freeze the final manuscript appendix with the row-operator convention, replace ambiguous matrix displays by U0,N,S10,S4, and then add D.1/D.2 pivot artifacts without reopening any new obstruction layer.[TURN75-NOTATION-STACKRANK-FIREWALL]
